raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to count html files under /testbed that contain string "foo". Use find and grep -l and wc -l. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.html' -exec grep -q 'foo' {} + -print? Actually need count. Use find ... -exec grep -l 'foo' {} + | wc -l. So final: find /testbed -type f -name '*.html' -exec grep -q 'foo' {} + -print? Wait -exec grep -l 'foo' {} + will output matching files. Then pipe to wc -l. So: find /testbed -type f -name '*.html' -exec grep -l 'foo' {} + | wc -l.
